title: Wet-Dry Crossover Field-State Features
summary: Public F1 strategy analysis and Pirelli material emphasize that the wet-to-intermediate-to-slick crossover is a major tactical trigger, often worth whole seconds per lap.
rationale: Public F1 strategy analysis and Pirelli material emphasize that the wet-to-intermediate-to-slick crossover is a major tactical trigger, often worth whole seconds per lap. The current feature set uses tyre labels, but not the field-level crossover pressure that appears when many cars are on different wet compounds and the track is drying.
implementation_hint: Per race-lap, compute field shares on WET/INTERMEDIATE/slicks, recent 1-2 lap transition counts between those classes, compound-specific median lap improvements, and a 'faster compound already proven' indicator relative to the current car. Interact those with position, tyre life, and race progress; optionally train a wet/changeable-race specialist.
expected_effect: Should add lift on rare but high-signal weather laps, where pit decisions are driven less by tyre age and more by crossover timing.
risk: Wet/changeable regimes are sparse and easy to overfit. Use heavy shrinkage, whole-race CV, and if the timing semantics are unclear, prefer lagged field-state aggregates.
